column1: Graphite is one of the softest materials known.
column2: Synthetic nanocrystalline diamond is the hardest material known.[47]

column1: Graphite is a very good lubricant, displaying superlubricity.[48]
Graphite is a conductor of electricity.[49]
column2: Diamond is an excellent electrical insulator,[50] and has the highest breakdown electric field of any known material.

column1: Some forms of graphite are used for thermal insulation (i.e. firebreaks and heat shields), but some other forms are good thermal conductors.
column2: Diamond is the best known naturally occurring thermal conductor.

column1: Graphite crystallizes in the hexagonal system.[51]
column2: Diamond crystallizes in the cubic system.
Carbon nanotubes are among the most anisotropic materials known.
